content:
The steam from the last batch of cheong fun is still clinging to my skin, but for the first time in fifteen years, it doesn't feel like a shroud. It feels like a parting gift.

I look at the calendar taped to the inside of the cart—the one where I used to meticulously mark every Saturday with a heavy, dutiful circle. This Saturday is blank. The white space on that square feels larger than the Paci
